content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Marvin Church 
County Clerk-Recorder 
County of San Mateo 
Hall of Justice and Records 
Redwood City, CA 
94063-0964 
Dear Mr. Church: 
June 25 f 
1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-86-183 
This is in response to your letter of May 22, 1986, and 
to confirm the telephone message I left with your office on 
June 4, 1986. 
You have asked whether Elections Code Section l2404(c) 
allows the campaign committee controlled by your incumbent 
sheriff, who passed away on April 21, 1986, to transfer its 
unused funds to the campaign of the acting sheriff. 
As I advised you on June 4, the Fair Political Practices 
Commission does not interpret or enforce the Elections Code. 
You should contact your district attorney's office or the 
Attorney General's office with regard to this matter. 
Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 
if you have additional questions. 
Sincerely, 
t!.ti\.lf<-'-te.Jv'I..£LL,l..£.d 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


BOARD OF SUPERVISOAS 
ANNA G. ESHOO 
TOM NOLAN 
WILLIAM J. SCHUMACHER 
K. JACQUELINE SPEIER 
JOHN M. WARD 
CLERK DIVISION 
WARREN A. SLOCUM 
HALL OF JUSTICE AND RECORDS 
REDWOOD CITY 
CALIFORNIA 94063·0964 
(415) 
Ms. Cardla Wardlow, PoHtical Reform Consultant 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street, SUite 800 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Ms. Wardlow: 
May 22, 1986 
OUr incumbent Sher1ff passed BWwy Aprn 21, 1986 and h1s C8mps1gn Comm1ttee has asked our off1ce 
about legal disbursement of the unused funds. They inquired as to whether or not they could cbnato to 
another C8mpsign or committee. 
In view of the fact that the elected officlal has left office through his psssing, it seems that Election 
Code 12404 (c) could be appl1ed and we have IDijsed the Comm1ttee of that code section. We WOUld, 
however t like your confirmation and approval that this is 8 correct procedure to foHow in this case. 
We would apprecfate your giving this matter priority attenUon as the Committee was to transfer the 
remaining funds to the Acting Sheriff who plans to run in our August 26. 1986 election. The 
petitions wnl be avall8ble in June under special1egisl8tion. Your cooperation 1n this matter 1s 
appreciated. 
~2t#~ 
Narvin Church 
County Clerk - Recort:Mr

sections.question: Whether Elections Code Section 12404(c) allows the campaign committee controlled by the incumbent sheriff, who passed away on April 21, 1986, to transfer its unused funds to the campaign of the acting sheriff.
sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission does not interpret or enforce the Elections Code. You should contact your district attorney's office or the Attorney General's office with regard to this matter.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an incumbent sheriff's campaign committee can transfer unused funds to the acting sheriff's campaign following the incumbent's death.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to answer and directed the requestor to contact the district attorney's office or Attorney General's office for interpretation of Elections Code Section 12404(c).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/referral letter. The FPPC explicitly states it does not interpret the Elections Code. Document includes both the incoming request (May 22, 1986) and FPPC response (June 25, 1986).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Elections Code Section 12404(c) allows the campaign committee controlled by the incumbent sheriff, who passed away on April 21, 1986, to transfer its unused funds to the campaign of the acting sheriff.

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission does not interpret or enforce the Elections Code. You should contact your district attorney's office or the Attorney General's office with regard to this matter.

embedding.summary: County Clerk Marvin Church requested FPPC advice on whether a deceased incumbent sheriff's campaign committee could transfer unused funds to the acting sheriff's campaign. The FPPC declined jurisdiction, stating it does not interpret the Elections Code and referred the matter to the district attorney or Attorney General.
